hild or Angavinikaya. The author says in
the-B-^. CVII 14 and Brhajjdtaka (jB.J.) XXVIII 6 that he has com-
posed Bahvdkaryam jdtakam (Natal astrology of many wonders),
Tdtrd (or Journey) and the P.S. that is Bahu codyam of many argu-
ments or wonders). His works are available in two versions, viz.,
Brhai (major) and Laghu (minor). His Brhad Tdtrd or Sr. Togqydtrd
was also known as Mahdydtrd. It had the commentary of SUryadeva
Somasut of Naidhruva Gotra. The Svalpaydtrd (Abridged work on
Journey) is a bo known as fika^ika Tdtrd.1 Another work on
"Journey,^ according to Utpala, is the Togqydtrd. Kern had edited
nine chapters of this work; and now the entire work has been
published. Bha^otpala refers2 to the two versions of the Vivdha-
patala and is reported to have commented on it. In B.S. 1.10 as
well as LI.7, the Acarya says that he has already written the Tdtrd.
Utpala invariably quotes from it and calls it Togaydtrd (under
XLIII.31).
